$comment: Path parsing for /vault/proxy/<service>/<upstream-path>. The 404-vs-400 boundary lives here: a missing prefix or malformed <service> is rejected at parse time (before any lease verification), while a valid parse with an unknown <service> falls through to the constant-time 404. service grammar: lower-case alnum + dot/dash/underscore, 1..63 chars.

vectors.name: happy_openai_chat
vectors.description: Canonical good shape — service + upstream-path, leading slash preserved on upstream-path.

vectors.name: happy_service_root_no_trailing_slash
vectors.description: /vault/proxy/<service> with no trailing slash and no upstream path. Returns ('<service>', '') — the empty string is the canonical 'service root' upstream path. Some upstreams treat / and empty as equivalent; downstream resolution handles that.

vectors.name: reject_wrong_prefix
vectors.description: Path doesn't start with /vault/proxy/ — caller is on the wrong route entirely. Reject at parse time (rather than fall through to a 404), so the route table can dispatch cleanly.

vectors.name: reject_service_uppercase
vectors.description: <service> must match [a-z0-9][a-z0-9._-]{0,62} — uppercase is rejected because case-sensitivity in service names would create lookup hazards (manifest entries are lowercase by convention; accepting OpenAI would either silently miss the openai entry or silently equate them, both bad).
